name: Twitter Financial Sentiment
description: Real-time sentiment scores for S&P 500 tickers derived from Twitter/X financial discourse.
longDescription: Twitter Financial Sentiment provides institutional investors with minute-level sentiment data extracted from public tweets mentioning S&P 500 companies. Each observation combines natural language processing across 100K+ daily financial tweets with market-relevant metadata like engagement metrics and user influence scores.
updateSchedule: Hourly, 15 minutes latency from tweet posting

fields.name: sentiment_score
fields.description: Sentiment polarity from -1 (very negative) to +1 (very positive), 0 = neutral

fields.name: sentiment_label
fields.description: Categorical sentiment: positive, negative, neutral, mixed

fields.name: tweet_volume
fields.description: Number of tweets in this hour matching ticker mention

fields.name: engagement_score
fields.description: Aggregated likes, retweets, replies normalized to 0-1 scale

fields.name: influencer_weight
fields.description: Weighted influence of contributors (higher = more influential users)

sla.revisionPolicy: Last 24 hours may be revised as more tweets are collected. Data older than 24h is finalized.

codeExamples.label: Python
codeExamples.code:
import requests
import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# Fetch hourly sentiment for AAPL
response = requests.get(
    'https://api.socialsent.com/v1/sentiment',
    params={
        'ticker': 'AAPL',
        'start_date': '2025-01-01',
        'end_date': '2025-01-15',
        'interval': 'hourly'
    },
    headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer YOUR_API_KEY'}
)

data = response.json()
df = pd.DataFrame(data['results'])
print(df[['date', 'ticker', 'sentiment_score', 'tweet_volume']].head())

codeExamples.label: SQL
codeExamples.code:
SELECT 
  date,
  hour,
  ticker,
  AVG(sentiment_score) as avg_sentiment,
  SUM(tweet_volume) as total_tweets,
  AVG(engagement_score) as avg_engagement
FROM twitter_sentiment
WHERE ticker IN ('AAPL', 'MSFT', 'GOOGL')
  AND date BETWEEN '2025-01-01' AND '2025-01-15'
GROUP BY date, hour, ticker
ORDER BY date DESC, hour DESC;

codeExamples.label: C#

lineage.description: Real-time tweets from public Twitter API filtered for S&P 500 ticker mentions (case-insensitive with validation)
FinBERT transformer model fine-tuned on financial discourse extracts polarity score and confidence confidence
Aggregates likes, retweets, replies, and quote-tweets; normalized across market hours vs after-hours
Scores user influence based on follower count, historical tweet virality, and verified status
Rolls up to hourly buckets per ticker; computes weighted averages and volume statistics
Outlier detection, null-handling, and consistency checks across tickers and time periods
Pushes to REST API, WebSocket stream, and S3 bulk export monthly snapshots

versionPolicy.deprecationPolicy: Major versions supported for 18 months. Minor versions forever. Patch versions for 90 days.
versionPolicy.breakingChanges: v2.0 (Jan 2024): Changed sentiment_score from -100 to +100 scale to -1 to +1 for consistency with NLP standards. Also added confidence field.
versionPolicy.revisionWindow: Last 24 hours may be revised as more tweets are collected and sentiment re-analyzed. Data older than 24h is immutable.

compliance.regulatoryClassification: Alternative Data - Non-PII derived sentiment signals from public social media
compliance.dataRetentionPolicy: All raw data retained for 24 months; aggregated data retained indefinitely for historical analysis

backtestGuide.sampleBacktest:
import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# Load hourly sentiment for backtesting
start_date = datetime(2023, 1, 1)
end_date = datetime(2024, 12, 31)

sent_data = fetch_sentiment(
    start_date=start_date,
    end_date=end_date,
    tickers=['AAPL', 'MSFT', 'GOOGL']
)

# Simple momentum strategy based on sentiment
sent_data['signal'] = (sent_data['sentiment_score'] > 0.3).astype(int)
sent_data['returns'] = sent_data.groupby('ticker')['price'].pct_change()
sent_data['strategy_returns'] = sent_data['signal'] * sent_data['returns']

print(f"Cumulative Return: {(1 + sent_data['strategy_returns']).cumprod().iloc[-1] - 1:.2%}")
print(f"Sharpe Ratio: {(sent_data['strategy_returns'].mean() / sent_data['strategy_returns'].std()) * 252:.2f}")
knownLimitations: Sentiment reflects social media discourse, not fundamental factors. High volume doesn't always imply material moves.
Bot tweets and spam may skew sentiment during low-volume periods for micro-cap stocks or meme stocks.
After-hours sentiment is less reliable due to lower participation; US-trading-hours tickers are most reliable.
Insider information and material news breaks typically flow through social media with 2-5 minute delay.
Negative sentiment bias: retail traders tend to tweet more during losses vs. gains (behavioral asymmetry).
backtestGuide.lookbackRequired: 90 days minimum to establish baseline sentiment regime and volatility regime for signal stability
backtestGuide.warmupPeriod: 30 days recommended to allow model to calibrate confidence thresholds and identify anomalies
